title: Shared Knowledge Boundary and Access Model
thesis: CTX should keep single-user runtime memory local and operational, while shared team knowledge remains an explicitly separate workspace layer with governance and non-goals documented before any enterprise execution work begins.

boundaries.title: Single-user runtime memory
boundaries.summary: The current runtime remains the canonical owner of pipeline state, session memory, local KB snapshots, and operator surfaces for one user in one workspace.
ownership: local knowledge artifacts and session exports
user-scoped provider setup and recovery controls
boundaries.writePath: Local runtime and repository-backed artifacts only.
boundaries.syncBoundary: No implicit cross-user sync; GitHub issues and explicit exports remain the durable handoff path.

boundaries.title: Shared team knowledge workspace
boundaries.summary: A future shared layer may expose curated knowledge, reusable templates, and governed decision artifacts, but it must sit above the single-user runtime rather than replacing it.
boundaries.writePath: Explicit publish/promote flows from local runtime artifacts into a shared workspace.
boundaries.syncBoundary: Shared knowledge sync is opt-in, reviewable, and policy-governed instead of automatic runtime replication.
excludedCapabilities: direct mutation of another user runtime state
hidden background replication from local KB to team storage

boundaries.title: Enterprise and deferred scope
boundaries.summary: Compliance, tenancy, marketplace, and community-scale collaboration remain deferred research tracks and must not leak requirements into the current Phase 0-2 runtime.
ownership: audit/legal controls beyond operator runtime needs
boundaries.writePath: No committed implementation path in the current roadmap.
boundaries.syncBoundary: Explicitly outside the single-user runtime and shared-team brief until a later ADR approves the direction.
excludedCapabilities: automatic schema requirements for today`s local KB

roles.title: Workspace owner
roles.summary: Owns membership, governance policy, and approval rules for what leaves the single-user runtime and becomes shared.
constraints: Must not bypass explicit publish/review boundaries for local runtime artifacts.
Owns policy defaults, not another user`s private runtime state.

roles.title: Contributor
roles.summary: Can propose or edit shared knowledge within workspace policy, but does not control governance rules.
constraints: Cannot change workspace membership or policy.
Export or publish actions can still require owner approval.

roles.title: Viewer
roles.summary: Can read approved shared knowledge and templates without mutating shared state.
constraints: No direct write or publish capability.
No access to another user`s local runtime state or credentials.

governanceAssumptions: Shared knowledge is promoted from local artifacts through explicit publish flows.
Local provider credentials and private runtime state never become shared workspace defaults.
Review and approval apply to shared exports/templates, not to a user`s private session loop.
rolloutGuards: Do not add team-write semantics to the current local KB or pipeline state machine.
Do not require enterprise tenancy or compliance design to finish current roadmap work.
Keep marketplace/community packaging as research until the shared workspace boundary proves useful.

nonGoals: Replacing the current single-user runtime with a multi-user state machine.
Sharing provider API keys, local model configs, or per-user recovery controls across a workspace.
Auto-syncing every local KB write into a shared database.
Committing to marketplace, tenant, or compliance features in the current build plan.
